# ADR-260010: Mobile-First Responsive Navigation with Hamburger Menu

## Workflow

- Drafted during responsive design planning for mobile navigation improvement.
- Stored as a standalone ADR and ready for implementation.

Status: Proposed
Date: 2026-04-08
Owners: Engineering

## Context

The current navigation menu displays all items horizontally, which works well on desktop but causes horizontal overflow and poor visual appearance on small screens (smartphones). Mobile users experience navigation items wrapping to multiple lines or requiring horizontal scrolling, creating a frustrating user experience.

The solution needs to maintain accessibility, preserve the existing desktop navigation behavior, and provide a clean mobile experience without over-engineering the implementation.

## Decision

- Implement a responsive navigation that transforms to a hamburger menu (☰) on screens smaller than 768px.
- On mobile, show the hamburger trigger in place of the horizontal menu bar.
- Tapping the hamburger reveals a full-screen or overlay vertical menu with all navigation items.
- Include the language switcher in the mobile menu if present.
- Use CSS-only transitions for menu open/close states to keep performance optimal.
- Maintain all accessibility features (ARIA labels, keyboard navigation, focus management) in the mobile implementation.
- Keep desktop navigation unchanged for screens ≥768px.

## Alternatives Considered

- Option A: Simply hide overflow and allow horizontal scrolling on mobile.
- Option B: Use a collapsible sidebar navigation that pushes content aside.
- Option C: Implement a bottom tab bar navigation for mobile.
- Option D: Responsive hamburger menu with overlay.

## Consequences

- Positive: Provides clean, accessible mobile navigation without horizontal overflow.
- Positive: Preserves desktop experience and maintains consistent branding.
- Positive: Lightweight implementation using existing Tailwind CSS and Astro components.
- Negative: Adds JavaScript for menu toggle state management.
- Risks: Menu overlay could interfere with page content if not properly layered.

## Verification

- Confirm navigation items fit within viewport on screens down to 320px.
- Confirm hamburger menu opens/closes properly and includes all navigation items.
- Confirm accessibility standards are maintained (WCAG compliance).
- Run responsive testing across device widths and browser compatibility.
- Verify no layout shifts or content interference on mobile.

## Rollback

- Remove mobile-specific CSS and JavaScript, reverting to horizontal menu across all screen sizes if issues arise.
